examples: The most powerful computers struggle to reliably recognize the shape of an object, the most elementary of tasks for a ten-month-old kid.
Economists who study the relationship between money and happiness have consistently found that, above a certain income, the two do not reliably correlate.
Birds store energy as subcutaneous fat deposits or "body reserves"; changes in these reserves can be reliably estimated by measuring changes in body mass.
But a new bipartisan survey indicates rural voters are not so reliably Republican in 2008.
If active management were a viable investment strategy, it should reliably outperform a purely passive index.
